title: Equivariant Symplectic Homology
speaker: Jean Gutt
abstract: Associated to a star-shaped domain in ℝ2nR2n are two increasing sequences of capacities: the Ekeland-Hofer capacities and the so-called Gutt-Hutchings capacities. I shall recall both constructions and then present the main theorem that they are the same. This is joint work with Vinicius Ramos.

proof_stages.label: Capacity landscape and theorem statement
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates the problem by reviewing symplectic capacities, their known coincidences and asymptotic relations, and then states the main theorem equating Ekeland-Hofer capacities with capacities from positive S^1-equivariant symplectic homology for star-shaped domains in R^{2n}.

proof_stages.label: Positive S^1-equivariant symplectic homology capacities
proof_stages.summary: For a smooth star-shaped domain, the boundary contact form, Reeb orbits, action filtration, nondegeneracy and grading data are introduced, leading to the definition of the capacity as the first filtration level at which the relevant degree of positive S^1-equivariant symplectic homology appears.

proof_stages.label: Ekeland-Hofer variational index and capacities
proof_stages.summary: The Ekeland-Hofer side is formulated on the H^{1/2} loop space using its Fourier splitting, the Hamiltonian action functional, the Fadell-Rabinowitz index, the special homeomorphism group Gamma, and the resulting min-max definition of Ekeland-Hofer capacities.

proof_stages.label: Hamiltonian-level reformulation
proof_stages.summary: Rather than comparing only after passing to a star-shaped domain, both theories are recast for a fixed admissible Hamiltonian, with Ekeland-Hofer capacities expressed through action sublevels and the homological capacities expressed through a filtered Floer quantity kappa_c.

proof_stages.label: Morse-Floer chain comparison in H^{1/2}
proof_stages.summary: The main technical input is a direct S^1-equivariant Morse theory for the Hamiltonian action functional on H^{1/2}, chain-isomorphic to the filtered S^1-equivariant Floer complex, yielding an interpretation of kappa_c as an equivariant index of the positive action window and clarifying the Euclidean/star-shaped scope of the argument.

proof_stages.label: Index inequalities and Gamma-controlled linear subspace
proof_stages.summary: Two comparison inequalities relate the Ekeland-Hofer index of an action sublevel to kappa_c, with the harder direction obtained by using Gamma and a Hofer-Zehnder-type linear-subspace index computation to place a sufficiently large positive-frequency subspace inside the required sublevel, completing the equality.
